ColorSlotTypes.brief: A set of constants defining color and style slot identifiers used for chat channels, score displays, and miscellaneous UI elements in the system.
ColorSlotTypes.details: The ColorSlotTypes group provides a structured collection of constexpr integer constants that serve as identifiers for various color and style slots within the application's user interface and messaging framework. These constants categorize different message types such as chat channels (e.g., gossip, flame, auction, social, private), score-related displays (including names, kill counts, class, stats, and other attributes), score decoration elements (like torch, flame, borders, and character info), and miscellaneous UI components (such as rooms, mobiles, objects, players, and inventory items). Each constant is associated with a specific index and typically includes default styling attributes like color and boldness, promoting visual consistency and ease of maintenance. By centralizing these identifiers, the system simplifies updates, extensions, and uniform application of styles across various modules and display functions.
ColorSlotTypes.notes: Constants are organized into categories reflecting their usage context, ensuring logical grouping and straightforward management. The use of descriptive naming conventions enhances code readability and maintainability, facilitating consistent styling and message formatting throughout the system.
ColorSlotTypes.rationale: This group exists to standardize and centralize the management of UI style and color schemes, enabling consistent visual cues and message formatting across different components of the application. By defining these identifiers in one place, it simplifies future modifications, supports theme or style updates, and ensures uniform user experience in chat, score displays, and miscellaneous UI elements.
ColorSlotTypes.name: ColorSlotTypes
